message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="luca-palmieri" color="blue" summary="Step 10 implementation ready for team review">
Team, Step 10 (Someday/Maybe, US-GTD-6) is committed locally (e4c7b73). Ready for review.

## Implementation Summary

### Domain: `SomedayMaybeItem` (struct, not enum)
- Simple struct with `id: SomedayMaybeId`, `user_id: UserId`, `title: TodoTitle`, `created_at: DateTime<Utc>`
- No state machine — items are created, edited, activated (moved to inbox), or deleted
- `SomedayMaybeId(Uuid)` newtype with the standard `new()`, `from_uuid()`, `as_uuid()` pattern
- 4 unit tests covering ID uniqueness, field access, from_parts reconstruction

### Database: `someday_maybe_items` table
- Migration `20240101000009`: `id`, `user_id` (FK to users), `title`, `created_at`
- Index on `user_id`

### Repository: `someday_maybe_repository.rs`
- `insert_someday_maybe_item(executor, &item)` — `impl PgExecutor` for transaction support
- `find_someday_maybe_items_by_user`, `find_someday_maybe_item_by_id`, `delete_someday_maybe_item`, `count_someday_maybe_items`, `update_someday_maybe_title`
- Also generalized `inbox_repository::insert_inbox_item` from `&PgPool` to `impl PgExecutor<'_>` (needed for the activate transaction)

### Service: `someday_maybe_service.rs`
- `add_someday_maybe_item` — validates title, persists
- `list_someday_maybe_items` — all items for user, ordered by created_at ASC
- `delete_someday_maybe_item` — ownership check, permanent removal
- `update_someday_maybe_title` — ownership check, title validation, persist
- `activate_someday_maybe_item` — transaction: creates InboxItem with same title, deletes SomedayMaybeItem

### Routes: `routes/someday_maybe.rs` (replaces placeholder)
- `GET /someday-maybe` — list page with add form, items, empty state
- `POST /someday-maybe` — add item (redirect / HTMX fragment)
- `POST /someday-maybe/{id}/activate` — move to inbox (redirect / HTMX empty body + announce "Moved to Inbox")
- `POST /someday-maybe/{id}/delete` — delete item (redirect / HTMX empty body)
- `GET /someday-maybe/{id}/edit` — inline edit form (HTMX)
- `POST /someday-maybe/{id}/edit` — submit edit (redirect / HTMX fragment)
- `GET /someday-maybe/{id}` — single item fragment (for cancel edit)

### Templates
- `someday_maybe.html` — list page with add form, empty state, item loop
- `someday_maybe_item.html` — item with Edit link, Activate button, Delete button
- `someday_maybe_edit.html` — inline edit form with Save button and Cancel link
- All actions have unique accessible `aria-label` including item title

### CSS: BEM `.someday-maybe*` classes in components layer
- Mirrors the waiting-for pattern with simpler structure (no "person" field)
- Uses design tokens throughout (no hardcoded values)

### Tests: 25 new integration tests
- Page loading (4): 200 status, heading, empty state, current page nav
- Add (3): redirect, empty title silently ignored, too-long title 422
- HTMX add (2): fragment + announce, empty title 204
- Activate (3): moves to inbox + redirect, HTMX empty body + announce, nonexistent 404
- Delete (3): redirect, HTMX empty body + announce, nonexistent 404
- Edit (2): redirect, HTMX fragment + announce
- A11y (3): accessible activate, delete, edit labels
- Semantic list (1): `<ul>` with `role="list"`
- User isolation (1): users cannot see each other's items
- Form label (1): visible label "Park an idea"
- HTMX edit form (1): inline form with Save/Cancel
- Single item (1): fragment for cancel edit

### Glossary: Updated with
- `SomedayMaybeItem`, `SomedayMaybeId` types (marked Implemented)
- 4 new actions: add, delete, edit, activate (all marked Implemented)
- 7 error types in Domain Errors table
- 3 error copy entries

### Pipeline: 252 Rust tests (89 unit + 163 integration) + 11 Playwright e2e, all green

Please review and provide your CONSENSUS: APPROVED or blocking feedback.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Let me also check the route handler for the announcement text and check if there's focus management JS.
No focus management JS for Someday/Maybe. Let me check if the Waiting For focus JS is being reused or if there's a shared approach.

No `{% block scripts %}` with a focus management JS file. The Waiting For feature had `waiting-for-focus.js` for WCAG 2.4.3 compliance. Someday/Maybe has the same interaction patterns (delete removes item, activate removes item, edit swaps inline form) but no focus management after those HTMX swaps. This is a potential issue.

input.content:
CONSENSUS: APPROVED with one item to flag (non-blocking for this step, but worth discussing). **UX review:** **Passes the trunk test:** - `current_page` set correctly, `aria-current="page"` on nav link - Clear `

Someday/Maybe

` heading - Descriptive page title "Someday/Maybe -- Todo List" **Good UX decisions:** 1. **"Park an idea"** as the add form label (line 27) -- friendly, low-commitment language that matches the list's purpose. Users understand they're not committing to anything. 2. **"Activate"** button with aria-labels including item title (e.g., "Activate: Learn Esperanto") -- clear and consistent. 3. **Announcement copy** -- "Idea parked" for add, "Moved to Inbox" for activate, "Item deleted" for delete, "Item updated" for edit. All user-language, all tell the user what happened. 4. **Placeholder** "e.g., Learn Esperanto" -- concrete, relatable, and sets the right tone for a someday list. 5. **Date display** with `` element -- consistent with Waiting For pattern. 6. **Edit form** -- clean inline pattern, consistent with other lists. **Empty state copy:** "No ideas parked yet. Capture anything you might want to do someday -- no commitment required." This is good -- the "no commitment required" phrasing lowers the barrier and explains the list's purpose. It's slightly different from what I suggested earlier but arguably better because "no commitment required" directly addresses the user's hesitation. **Missing page subtitle** (Minor, non-blocking): I suggested a subtitle under the h1 like "Ideas and someday projects" to explain the GTD jargon. It's not there, and honestly the empty state and form label ("Park an idea") do a good job explaining the concept on their own. I won't block on this. **Focus management JS** (flagging for team discussion): There's no `someday-maybe-focus.js` equivalent. Waiting For had `waiting-for-focus.js` for WCAG 2.4.3 compliance -- handling focus after delete/activate removes an item from the DOM, and after edit mode transitions. Someday/Maybe has identical interaction patterns (activate removes item, delete removes item, edit swaps inline). Without focus management, after activating or deleting an item, focus goes to ``. Heydon should weigh in on whether this needs to be addressed now or can be deferred. From a pure usability standpoint, losing focus is disorienting, but I defer to the a11y specialist on whether this is blocking. No other concerns. Ship it.
input.summary: Approved — good UX, flagged missing focus management JS for team discussion

content.text:
Step 10 approved. The UX is clean -- "Park an idea" as a label, "no commitment required" in the empty state, and "Activate" as the action verb all hit the right tone for a low-pressure idea parking lot.

I flagged one item for team discussion: there's no focus management JS for Someday/Maybe, unlike Waiting For which had dedicated focus handling. The interaction patterns are identical (items get removed from DOM on activate/delete), so focus would be lost to `<body>`. Deferring to Heydon on whether that's blocking from an a11y perspective.
